intro: For the one who inspires me the most.

poems.title: The Patio
poems.body:
I want to sit on my patio listening to the wind,
As I rock in my chair thinking about the woman I would never find.
I pour a glass of 20 year old Burgundy into my lonely glass,
As it swirls I can't help but think about how the time will pass.
What good is this moment and how meaningful is my life,
If I can't share these cherry notes I am enjoying if I don't have a wife.
The clouds turn grey and the leaves on the trees howl even louder,
The calm I was sitting in all of a sudden begins to scatter.
I must make the changes to fend off this loneliness,
I must put myself out there to find my happiness.
The next day brings a golden sky in the valley,
I pack my bag and head down a new alley.
As I sit down at a wine bar full of song and dance that I had never explored,
I scan the room expecting the emptiness to which I had so often been accustomed.
I rise out of my chair as my heart unexpectedly comes to despair,
My gaze locks on this maiden with shamrock green eyes and auburn hair.
I firmly plant myself back on my seat,
I try not to stare but I can't help but feel the weight of this feat.
I sat alone and quiet amongst the sound of music,
Hoping she would find me in the loudness of the acoustic.
I raised my hand to order another drink,
As the maiden caught my eyes she raised two fingers and gave me a wink.
Before the waitress had even poured the round,
This maiden was sitting across from me as if to say look what I have found.
Though the atmosphere around was chaotic and vibrant,
Our conversation was mild and full of wonderment.
My glass was no longer alone and full of despair,
It was alive and accompanied as the aromas of our Sancerre.
We were lost in ourselves and in infatuation,
Giving each other the eye to take this moment to fruition.
I raised my hand and paid the tab,
We slowly walked out of the establishment hand in hand.
We wandered all the way to my place,
Never had such a wonderful maiden filled my space.
Now I sit on my patio listening to the wind,
Knowing immediately she was one of a kind.
